macro_summary: US stocks edged higher Friday with SPY up 0.11% and QQQ up 0.94% as hyperscaler capex commitments and Azure revenue beat offset Hormuz uncertainty. Trump's plan to guide ships through Hormuz eased supply fears, though WTI still held at $101.81. The Fed kept rates on hold, pushing any cut to late 2026.
commodities_note: WTI steadied at $101.81 (-0.13%) as naval escort plan reduced blockade risk. Gold eased 0.74% to $4,610. US10Y dipped to 4.38% (-0.27%). DXY flat at 98.19.

singapore.summary: Singapore banks remain bifurcated: DBS blocked on RSI, while OCBC and UOB hold with earnings this week. No NIM relief yet as US10Y at 4.38% above the 4.20% reset threshold. The cluster is in wait-and-see mode ahead of the earnings releases.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired by dual NIM compression; awaiting earnings clarity.
singapore.key_development: OCBC reports May 8 and UOB May 7, which could break the HOLD deadlock if margins stabilize.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX surged 1.73% to 465.75 but mega-cap semis are all deeply extended and hard-blocked. ASML sits near its SMA50 with a workable 2.1:1 R:R, making it the only actionable name. Micron's +27% extension and AVGO's +21% leave no room for entry, while AMD (+53%) and Intel (+79%) are in uncharted territory.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically blocked by extreme extension — only ASML offers a window.
semiconductors.key_development: ASML's High-NA EUV delay by TSMC is a timing concern, but the overall semi demand backdrop from $630B in hyperscaler capex remains robust.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is uniformly extended with AMZN (+19%) and GOOG (+22%) hitting extreme thresholds. MSFT downgraded to HOLD after the OpenAI deal restructure compressed its R:R, and PLTR faces an earnings binary tonight. No names are actionable; the cluster is in waiting mode for pullbacks or post-earnings resets.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally mixed — capex boom vs. OpenAI concentration risk; tactically extended.
big_tech.key_development: Microsoft's Azure +40% and the OpenAI revenue cap (CNBC) highlight the dependency risk while PLTR's defense AI narrative faces its next test tonight.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics partner — any NVDA datacenter expansion or AI optics news directly lifts COHR's order book.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 8-10% from current levels on sector rotation or after LITE's May 6 earnings.

interconnected.name: Dell Technologies
interconnected.reason: Hyperscaler $630B capex means massive AI server orders; Dell is a top supplier alongside NVDA GPUs.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after MSFT's next quarter if Azure ex-OpenAI RPO accelerates, signaling organic server demand.

interconnected.name: Meta Platforms
interconnected.reason: $115-135B 2026 capex fuels the AI infrastructure boom, and its custom chip shift (canceling Olympus, renting TPU) impacts GOOG and AVGO.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Meta's Q2 earnings show AI-driven ad revenue growth above 20%.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted despite US naval escort plan — shipping has not normalized, keeping WTI above $100.
US-China tech trade war escalates with sanctions on COMAC aircraft and port fees on Chinese-built vessels, pressuring global supply chains.
Federal Reserve holds rates at 3.50%-3.75% with no clear timeline for cuts, maintaining hawkish pressure on growth stocks.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $101 supports elevated energy costs and sticky inflation, keeping Fed on hold. Singapore banks (DBS, OCBC, UOB) suffer NIM compression, while semis (NVDA, AMD, etc.) remain extended as the AI capex story outweighs macro drag. Defense AI (PLTR) benefits but earnings tonight are the binary.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump announced the US will 'guide' ships through Hormuz, easing immediate blockade fears; WTI edged lower but stays above $100.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Selective entry on ASML and WRD where risk/reward is favourable; avoid all extended semis and wait for earnings resolutions on Singapore banks and BigTech.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously bullish with tactical entry only
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 11 of 16 names are hard-blocked on technical extension; the few actionable entries require strict discipline as the Hormuz crisis and hawkish Fed dominate the macro backdrop.
